question: Do human-framed packets improve downstream agent next-step work compared with task-only and evidence-only prompts?

A_task_only.description: Task question plus bounded source anchors only.

B_evidence_only.description: Task question plus machine-readable evidence/status/gap/action ledgers without human framing.

C_human_framed.description: Same machine-readable payload as B plus the human-framed packet reasoning.

fairness_controls.identical_response_length_band: 300-700 words unless the subject model uses structured bullets that are equivalent in content.
fairness_controls.identical_retry_policy: One response per prompt. No hidden retries. Malformed responses are recorded, not replaced, unless a predeclared transport failure occurs.
fairness_controls.source_excerpt_policy: No large raw source excerpts. Use bounded source anchors and summaries already present in packets.
fairness_controls.b_c_machine_evidence_parity: Condition C may add human framing and answer_text; all non-framing machine evidence available to C must also be available to B.
fairness_controls.condition_ordering_policy: Response collection should randomize or counterbalance case/condition ordering if the execution path permits; otherwise preserve manifest order and record that limitation.

model_use_gate.if_approval_missing: write blocked_no_model_use_approval result and stop before scoring

hard_veto_policy.summary_rule: Report hard vetoes before per-dimension scores. A response or condition with hard-veto regression cannot be declared improved by explanatory dimensions.

non_claims: This is not a public benchmark.
This is not proof of general downstream-agent reliability.
This does not prove any mathematical claim.
This does not prove the packet standard is universally optimal.
